nodes.label: SBA 7(a)
nodes.definition: A loan program offered by the Small Business Administration to assist small businesses with various financing needs.

nodes.label: Borrower FAQ
nodes.definition: Frequently asked questions that provide guidance to borrowers regarding the SBA 7(a) loan program.

nodes.label: SBA Express
nodes.definition: A streamlined loan program that allows approved lenders to make loans of up to $500,000 with a 36-hour SBA turnaround.

nodes.label: Community Advantage
nodes.definition: A pilot program that allows mission-focused lenders to make SBA 7(a) loans of up to $350,000 targeting underserved communities.

nodes.label: For-Profit Business
nodes.definition: The borrower must operate a for-profit small business in the United States.

nodes.label: SBA Size Standards
nodes.definition: The criteria that define a small business according to the SBA, which vary by industry based on the number of employees or average annual receipts.

nodes.label: Ability to Repay
nodes.definition: The borrower must demonstrate the ability to repay the loan.

nodes.label: Sound Business Purpose
nodes.definition: The borrower must have a sound business purpose for the loan.

nodes.label: Credit Elsewhere
nodes.definition: The borrower must show that they cannot obtain credit elsewhere on reasonable terms.

nodes.label: No Federal Debt Delinquency
nodes.definition: The borrower must not be delinquent on any federal debt.

nodes.label: Loan Guarantee
nodes.definition: The SBA guarantees a portion of the loan, reducing lender risk.

nodes.label: Interest Rate Cap
nodes.definition: Interest rates for SBA loans are capped by SBA regulations.

nodes.label: Repayment Term
nodes.definition: The loan can have longer repayment terms, generally up to 25 years for real estate.

nodes.label: Down Payment Requirement
nodes.definition: The SBA may have lower down payment requirements for borrowers.

nodes.label: Maximum Loan Amount
nodes.definition: The maximum loan amount for a standard SBA 7(a) loan is $5,000,000.

nodes.label: Maximum SBA Express Loan Amount
nodes.definition: The maximum loan amount for an SBA Express loan is $500,000.

nodes.label: Maximum Community Advantage Loan Amount
nodes.definition: The maximum loan amount for a Community Advantage loan is $350,000.

nodes.label: SBA-approved lender
nodes.definition: A lender that is approved by the Small Business Administration to offer SBA loans.

nodes.label: Business Plan
nodes.definition: A document outlining the business strategy, required for startups or significant changes.

nodes.label: Financial Documents
nodes.definition: Documents that provide an overview of the financial status of a business, including income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.

nodes.label: SBA Lender Match Tool
nodes.definition: A tool provided by the SBA to help borrowers find approved lenders.

nodes.label: SBA Loan Program
nodes.definition: A program offered by the Small Business Administration that may require personal guarantees from business owners.

nodes.label: Application Documentation
nodes.definition: Documents required to be submitted with the loan application.

nodes.label: Lender Evaluation
nodes.definition: The process by which a lender assesses the borrower's application.

nodes.label: SBA Approval
nodes.definition: Approval required from the SBA for certain actions, such as changes in ownership or major loan modifications.

nodes.label: Preferred Lenders Program (PLP) Loan
nodes.definition: A loan program through preferred lenders that offers expedited processing.

nodes.label: Standard 7(a) Loan
nodes.definition: A traditional SBA loan program with standard processing times.

nodes.label: Credit Score Requirement
nodes.definition: The requirement that lenders may have their own standards for credit scores, affecting loan approval.

nodes.label: Minimum Credit Score
nodes.definition: The minimum credit score used by the SBA's pre-screening tool (FICO SBSS).

nodes.label: Personal Financial Statements
nodes.definition: Financial statements required from all owners with 20% or more ownership.

nodes.label: Business Financial Statements
nodes.definition: Financial documents including tax returns, profit & loss statements, and balance sheets.

nodes.label: Schedule of Business Debt
nodes.definition: A document listing all current business debts.

nodes.label: Resumes for Principals
nodes.definition: Resumes required for each principal involved in the business.

nodes.label: Business Licenses and Permits
nodes.definition: Required licenses and permits for operating the business.

nodes.label: Lease Agreements
nodes.definition: Agreements for leasing property, if applicable.

nodes.label: Articles of Incorporation
nodes.definition: Legal documents establishing the existence of a corporation.

nodes.label: Loan Processing Time
nodes.definition: The duration it takes to process a loan application.

nodes.label: Preferred Lender Processing Time
nodes.definition: The time taken for loan processing through preferred lenders.

nodes.label: Standard Lender Processing Time
nodes.definition: The time taken for loan processing through standard lenders.

nodes.label: Total Process Time
nodes.definition: The total time from application to funding.

nodes.label: FICO SBSS
nodes.definition: The credit scoring model used by the SBA for pre-screening loan applications.

nodes.label: Credit Score of 680
nodes.definition: The preferred personal credit score for most lenders.

nodes.label: Minimum FICO SBSS Score
nodes.definition: The minimum score of 155 out of 300 used by the SBA's credit scoring model.

nodes.label: Interest Rate
nodes.definition: The percentage of a loan amount that a lender charges as interest to the borrower.

nodes.label: Maximum Interest Rate for Loans Over $350,000
nodes.definition: The maximum interest rate for loans over $350,000, which is prime plus 3%.

nodes.label: Maximum Interest Rate for Loans Under $50,000
nodes.definition: The maximum interest rate for loans under $50,000, which can be up to prime plus 6.5%.

nodes.label: SBA Guaranty Fee
nodes.definition: A fee that ranges from 2% to 3.5% of the guaranteed portion of the loan, depending on the loan amount.

nodes.label: Annual Service Fee
nodes.definition: A fee of 0.55% of the outstanding balance of SBA's guaranteed portion of each loan.

nodes.label: Fee Reduction Programs
nodes.definition: Programs offered by the SBA that provide fee relief for certain borrowers, such as veterans and military spouses.

nodes.label: Closing Costs
nodes.definition: Reasonable costs charged by lenders for closing a loan, which may vary.

nodes.label: Prohibited Fees
nodes.definition: Certain fees that the SBA prohibits, including bonus points, brokerage fees, and other unreasonable charges.

nodes.label: Working Capital
nodes.definition: Funds used for the day-to-day operations of a business.

nodes.label: Equipment
nodes.definition: Funds used to purchase equipment for business operations.

nodes.label: Real Estate
nodes.definition: Funds used to purchase real estate for business purposes.

nodes.label: Business Acquisition
nodes.definition: Funds used to acquire an existing business.

nodes.label: Repayment Terms
nodes.definition: Conditions under which a loan must be repaid.

nodes.label: Prepayment Penalty
nodes.definition: A fee charged for paying off a loan early.

nodes.label: Default Procedure
nodes.definition: The process followed when a borrower defaults on a loan.

nodes.label: Loan Maturity 15 Years or More
nodes.definition: Loans with a maturity of 15 years or more.

nodes.label: Loan Maturity Less Than 15 Years
nodes.definition: Loans with a maturity of less than 15 years.

nodes.label: Year 1 Prepayment Penalty
nodes.definition: 5% penalty on the amount prepaid during the first year.

nodes.label: Year 2 Prepayment Penalty
nodes.definition: 3% penalty on the amount prepaid during the second year.

nodes.label: Year 3 Prepayment Penalty
nodes.definition: 1% penalty on the amount prepaid during the third year.

nodes.label: No Penalty After Year 3
nodes.definition: No prepayment penalty after the third year.

nodes.label: Credit Score Impact
nodes.definition: The effect of defaulting on a loan on the borrower's credit score.

nodes.label: Forgiveness Eligibility
nodes.definition: Conditions under which a loan may be forgiven.

nodes.label: Export Express
nodes.definition: A loan program providing up to $500,000 to small businesses for export activities, with varying SBA guarantees.

nodes.label: SBA Microloans
nodes.definition: Loans of up to $50,000 through intermediary lenders, suitable for startups.

nodes.label: Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs)
nodes.definition: Mission-focused lenders that provide loans and support to underserved communities.
The maximum loan amount for SBA Express and Export Express programs.
The maximum loan amount for the Community Advantage program.
The maximum loan amount for SBA Microloans.

nodes.label: 50% Guarantee
nodes.definition: The percentage of loan amount guaranteed by the SBA for SBA Express loans.

nodes.label: 90% Guarantee
nodes.definition: The percentage of loan amount guaranteed by the SBA for Export Express loans of $350,000 or less.

nodes.label: 75% Guarantee
nodes.definition: The percentage of loan amount guaranteed by the SBA for Export Express loans over $350,000.

nodes.label: Strong Business Plan
nodes.definition: A requirement for startups to obtain SBA loans.

nodes.label: Relevant Industry Experience
nodes.definition: A requirement for startups to obtain SBA loans.

nodes.label: Good Personal Credit
nodes.definition: A requirement for startups to obtain SBA loans.

nodes.label: Collateral or Personal Equity Contribution
nodes.definition: A requirement for startups to obtain SBA loans.

nodes.label: SBA Microloans
nodes.definition: A loan program that offers smaller loan amounts with potentially more flexible requirements for small businesses.

nodes.label: Collateral Requirement
nodes.definition: The requirement for lenders to take available collateral for loans, though not solely a reason for denial.

nodes.label: Loan Amount Up to $25,000
nodes.definition: The maximum loan amount for which no collateral is required.

nodes.label: Credit Score Below 650
nodes.definition: A condition where borrowers may have difficulty finding an approved lender willing to make the loan.

nodes.label: Approved Lender
nodes.definition: Banks, credit unions, or other institutions approved to lend SBA-backed loans.

nodes.label: SBA Regulation
nodes.definition: The set of rules established by the SBA that govern the lending process.

nodes.label: Public Program Guide
nodes.definition: A guide published by the U.S. Small Business Administration outlining the details of various loan programs.

nodes.label: Guaranty Percentage
nodes.definition: The percentage of the loan that the SBA guarantees.

nodes.label: Loan Amount
nodes.definition: The amount of money that can be borrowed under the SBA 7(a) Loan Program.

nodes.label: Partnering Lenders
nodes.definition: Lenders that partner with the SBA to provide loans under the 7(a) Loan Program.

nodes.label: Community Development Organizations
nodes.definition: Organizations that provide loans under the SBA 7(a) Loan Program.

nodes.label: Micro-Lending Institutions
nodes.definition: Institutions that provide small loans under the SBA 7(a) Loan Program.

nodes.label: Risk Elimination
nodes.definition: The reduction of risk to lending partners due to SBA loan guarantees.

nodes.label: Eligibility Requirements
nodes.definition: Criteria that businesses must meet to qualify for the SBA 7(a) loan.

nodes.label: For-Profit Requirement
nodes.definition: The business must be a for-profit entity operating in the United States or its territories.

nodes.label: Small Business
nodes.definition: A business that meets the SBA's size standards for eligibility in various loan programs.

nodes.label: Manufacturing Size Limit
nodes.definition: Manufacturing businesses can have up to 500 employees to qualify as a small business.

nodes.label: Service Business Receipts Limit
nodes.definition: Service businesses can have average annual receipts up to $8 million to qualify as a small business.

nodes.label: NAICS Code Variation
nodes.definition: The thresholds for small business size standards that vary by specific NAICS code.

nodes.label: Business Engagement Requirement
nodes.definition: The requirement for a business to be engaged in or propose to do business in the United States or its territories.

nodes.label: Reasonable Invested Equity
nodes.definition: The business owner must have invested their own time and/or money into the business, demonstrating a personal stake in its success.

nodes.label: Use Alternative Financial Resources
nodes.definition: The requirement for borrowers to utilize personal assets and other financial resources before seeking SBA assistance.

nodes.label: Demonstrate Inability to Obtain Non-Federal Credit
nodes.definition: The requirement for borrowers to show they cannot obtain credit on reasonable terms from non-Federal sources.

nodes.label: Demonstrate Need for Loan Proceeds
nodes.definition: The requirement for a business to show a valid purpose for the loan funds.

nodes.label: Delinquent on Existing Debt Obligations
nodes.definition: The requirement that the borrower must not be delinquent on any existing debt obligations to the U.S. government.

nodes.label: Federal Debts
nodes.definition: Includes student loans, prior SBA loans, and other federal debts that must be current.

nodes.label: Ineligible Businesses
nodes.definition: Types of businesses that are generally not eligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Illegal Activities
nodes.definition: Businesses engaged in illegal activities are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Discriminatory Practices
nodes.definition: Businesses that restrict patronage based on race, color, religion, or national origin are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Gambling Enterprises
nodes.definition: Gambling enterprises are generally ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans, except in certain states where gambling is legal.

nodes.label: Lending Businesses
nodes.definition: Businesses primarily engaged in lending, such as banks and finance companies, are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Speculative Activities
nodes.definition: Businesses primarily engaged in speculative activities are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Pyramid Sale Distribution Plans
nodes.definition: Pyramid sale distribution plans are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Promotion of Religion
nodes.definition: Businesses engaged in the promotion of religion are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Government-Owned Entities
nodes.definition: Government-owned entities, except Native American tribes, are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Religious Instruction
nodes.definition: Businesses principally engaged in teaching or instructing religion are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Private Clubs
nodes.definition: Private clubs that limit membership are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Life Insurance Companies
nodes.definition: Life insurance companies are ineligible for SBA 7(a) loans.

nodes.label: Standard 7(a) Loan
nodes.definition: A loan program that provides financial assistance to small businesses.

nodes.label: Export Working Capital
nodes.definition: A loan program that provides working capital for exporters.

nodes.label: International Trade
nodes.definition: A loan program that supports small businesses involved in international trade.
The maximum loan amount for certain SBA loan programs.
The maximum loan amount for SBA Express and Export Express.
Loans that require adherence to established collateral policies.
A threshold amount for determining SBA guaranty percentages.

nodes.label: 85% Guaranty
nodes.definition: The percentage of the loan amount that is guaranteed by the SBA for loans of $150,000 or less.

nodes.label: 75% Guaranty
nodes.definition: The percentage of the loan amount that is guaranteed by the SBA for loans greater than $150,000.

nodes.label: 50% Guaranty
nodes.definition: The percentage of the loan amount that is guaranteed by the SBA for SBA Express loans.

nodes.label: 10 Years
nodes.definition: The general maturity term for working capital and inventory loans.

nodes.label: 25 Years
nodes.definition: The maximum maturity term for real estate loans.

nodes.label: Mixed-Use Maturity
nodes.definition: A blended maturity term based on the use of proceeds for mixed-use loans.

nodes.label: Fixed Rate Loans ($50,000 or less)
nodes.definition: Loans of $50,000 or less with a fixed interest rate of Prime rate plus 6.5%.

nodes.label: Variable Rate Loans ($50,000 or less)
nodes.definition: Loans of $50,000 or less with a variable interest rate of Prime rate plus 6.5%.

nodes.label: Fixed Rate Loans ($50,001 - $250,000)
nodes.definition: Loans between $50,001 and $250,000 with a fixed interest rate of Prime rate plus 6.0%.

nodes.label: Variable Rate Loans ($50,001 - $250,000)
nodes.definition: Loans between $50,001 and $250,000 with a variable interest rate of Prime rate plus 6.0%.

nodes.label: Fixed Rate Loans ($250,001 - $350,000)
nodes.definition: Loans between $250,001 and $350,000 with a fixed interest rate of Prime rate plus 4.5%.

nodes.label: Variable Rate Loans ($250,001 - $350,000)
nodes.definition: Loans between $250,001 and $350,000 with a variable interest rate of Prime rate plus 4.5%.

nodes.label: Fixed Rate Loans (greater than $350,000)
nodes.definition: Loans greater than $350,000 with a fixed interest rate of Prime rate plus 3.0%.

nodes.label: Variable Rate Loans (greater than $350,000)
nodes.definition: Loans greater than $350,000 with a variable interest rate of Prime rate plus 3.0%.
The maximum loan amount for specific interest rate terms.
The maximum loan amount for specific interest rate terms.
The maximum loan amount for specific interest rate terms.

nodes.label: Greater than $350,000
nodes.definition: The loan amount category for loans exceeding $350,000.

nodes.label: SBA Guaranty Fees
nodes.definition: Fees paid by lenders to the SBA for each loan approved, typically passed on to the borrower.

nodes.label: Loans of $150,000 or less
nodes.definition: Loan amount category for which a 2.0% fee is applied.

nodes.label: Loans of $150,001 to $700,000
nodes.definition: Loan amount category for which a 3.0% fee is applied.

nodes.label: Loans of $700,001 to $5,000,000
nodes.definition: Loan amount category for which a 3.5% fee plus an additional 0.25% fee is applied.

nodes.label: Guaranteed Portion Fee
nodes.definition: The fee structure based on the guaranteed portion of the loan.

nodes.label: 2.0% Fee
nodes.definition: Fee percentage applied to loans of $150,000 or less.

nodes.label: 3.0% Fee
nodes.definition: Fee percentage applied to loans between $150,001 and $700,000.

nodes.label: 3.5% Fee plus 0.25%
nodes.definition: Fee structure for loans between $700,001 and $5,000,000.

nodes.label: 0.55% Annual Service Fee
nodes.definition: Percentage of the outstanding balance charged as an annual service fee.

nodes.label: Machinery and Equipment
nodes.definition: Funds allocated for purchasing machinery and equipment.

nodes.label: Furniture and Fixtures
nodes.definition: Funds used for acquiring furniture and fixtures.

nodes.label: Land and Building
nodes.definition: Funds for purchasing, renovating, or constructing land and buildings.

nodes.label: Leasehold Improvements
nodes.definition: Funds used for improvements made to leased property.

nodes.label: Debt Refinancing
nodes.definition: Funds used to refinance existing debt under certain conditions.

nodes.label: Seasonal Line of Credit
nodes.definition: Funds available for seasonal business needs.

nodes.label: Inventory
nodes.definition: Funds used for purchasing inventory.

nodes.label: Starting a New Business or Acquisition
nodes.definition: Funds used to start a new business or acquire an existing one.

nodes.label: Floor Plan Financing
nodes.definition: Financing for purchasing inventory that is held for sale.

nodes.label: Investment in Rental Real Estate
nodes.definition: Investments in rental properties without active borrower involvement.

nodes.label: Speculative Purposes
nodes.definition: Funds used for speculative investments.

nodes.label: Lending Activities
nodes.definition: Funds used for lending to others.

nodes.label: Pyramid Sales Plans
nodes.definition: Funds used for pyramid scheme activities.

nodes.label: Delinquent Federal or State Taxes
nodes.definition: Funds used to pay overdue taxes.

nodes.label: Repaying Debt to SBIC
nodes.definition: Funds used to repay debts owed to Small Business Investment Companies.

nodes.label: Insufficient Collateral Policy
nodes.definition: The policy stating that a loan will not be declined solely due to insufficient collateral.

nodes.label: Lien on Business Assets
nodes.definition: A requirement for the borrower to provide a lien on all available business assets as collateral for the loan.

nodes.label: Lien on Personal Assets
nodes.definition: A requirement for the borrower to provide a lien on personal assets as collateral for the loan.

nodes.label: Loan Amount Over $350,000
nodes.definition: Loans that must be collateralized to the maximum extent possible.

nodes.label: Personal Guaranty
nodes.definition: A requirement for all owners of 20% or more of the business to provide a personal guaranty, and potentially others in key management positions.

nodes.label: Owner 20% or More
nodes.definition: A requirement that all owners of 20% or more of the business must provide a personal guaranty.

nodes.label: SBA Approval Loan Program
nodes.definition: Loans that require approval from the Small Business Administration (SBA) before disbursement.

nodes.label: Lender
nodes.definition: An entity that provides loans to borrowers, often requiring approval for certain loan programs.

nodes.label: SBA Oversight
nodes.definition: The oversight provided by the Small Business Administration (SBA) for loans.

nodes.label: Lender Services Loan
nodes.definition: The type of lender that services the loan.

nodes.label: SBA-approved Lenders
nodes.definition: Lenders that are approved by the SBA to participate in its loan programs.

nodes.label: SBA Standard Operating Procedures
nodes.definition: Guidelines established by the SBA that lenders must follow.

nodes.label: SOP 50 10
nodes.definition: Standard Operating Procedure for Lender and Development Company Loan Programs.

nodes.label: SOP 50 57
nodes.definition: Standard Operating Procedure for 7(a) Loan Servicing and Liquidation.

nodes.label: Lender Eligibility
nodes.definition: Criteria that lenders must meet to participate in the SBA 7(a) loan program.

nodes.label: Participation Guidelines
nodes.definition: Guidelines that outline how lenders can participate in the SBA 7(a) loan program.

nodes.label: U.S. Small Business Administration
nodes.definition: The federal agency that administers the SBA 7(a) loan program.

nodes.label: Lender Program Guide
nodes.definition: A guide provided by the U.S. Small Business Administration detailing the rules and requirements for lenders.

nodes.label: Delegation Levels
nodes.definition: Different levels of responsibilities and authorities that lenders have when participating in the SBA 7(a) program.

nodes.label: SBA Lenders
nodes.definition: Lenders that are authorized to provide loans under SBA programs.

nodes.label: Regular Lenders
nodes.definition: Lenders that submit all loan applications to the SBA for approval.

nodes.label: SBA Approval Process
nodes.definition: The process by which the SBA reviews loan applications submitted by regular lenders.

nodes.label: Federally Regulated Bank
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to be federally regulated banks.

nodes.label: Satisfactory CRA Rating
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to have a satisfactory Community Reinvestment Act rating.

nodes.label: SBA Lender Training
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to complete SBA lender training.

nodes.label: Loan Authorization and Lender Agreement
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to sign a Loan Authorization and Lender Agreement.

nodes.label: Adequate Lending Experience
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to demonstrate adequate lending experience.

nodes.label: 5-10 Business Days
nodes.definition: The typical time frame for the SBA approval process.

nodes.label: Certified Lenders Program (CLP)
nodes.definition: A loan program where lenders receive partial delegation from the SBA to prepare credit analyses, which are then expedited for SBA review.

nodes.label: Regular Lender Requirements
nodes.definition: The baseline requirements that lenders must meet to qualify as Regular SBA lenders.

nodes.label: Minimum 12 Months as Regular SBA Lender
nodes.definition: A requirement for CLP status that mandates lenders must have been a Regular SBA lender for at least 12 months.

nodes.label: Minimum Portfolio of 10 SBA Loans
nodes.definition: A requirement for CLP status that mandates lenders must have a portfolio of at least 10 SBA loans.

nodes.label: Satisfactory SBA Loan Performance
nodes.definition: A requirement for CLP status that mandates a default rate below the SBA average.

nodes.label: Demonstrated Proficiency in SBA Lending
nodes.definition: A requirement for CLP status that mandates lenders demonstrate proficiency in SBA lending.

nodes.label: Preferred Lenders Program (PLP)
nodes.definition: A loan program that allows lenders to make credit decisions independently without prior SBA approval, considered the gold standard in SBA lending.

nodes.label: CLP Requirements
nodes.definition: Requirements that must be met to achieve CLP status, which are also applicable to PLP status.

nodes.label: Minimum 24 Months as a CLP Lender
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must have been a CLP lender for at least 24 months.

nodes.label: Minimum Portfolio of 20 SBA Loans
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must have a portfolio of at least 20 SBA loans.

nodes.label: Excellent Loan Performance History
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must demonstrate a history of excellent loan performance.

nodes.label: Demonstrated Knowledge of SBA Policies
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must show thorough knowledge of SBA policies and procedures.

nodes.label: Adequate Internal Controls
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must have adequate internal controls and compliance systems.

nodes.label: Regular SBA Audits and Reviews
nodes.definition: A requirement for PLP status that mandates lenders must undergo regular SBA audits and reviews.

nodes.label: Fast Processing Times
nodes.definition: A benefit of PLP status that allows for the fastest processing times with credit decisions made in-house.

nodes.label: Greater Operational Flexibility
nodes.definition: A benefit of PLP status that provides lenders with greater operational flexibility.

nodes.label: Competitive Advantage
nodes.definition: A benefit of PLP status that gives lenders a competitive advantage in serving borrowers.

nodes.label: Reduced SBA Oversight
nodes.definition: A benefit of PLP status that results in reduced SBA oversight for routine decisions.

nodes.label: Credit Analysis Procedures
nodes.definition: The procedures that lenders use to assess the creditworthiness of borrowers.

nodes.label: Documentation
nodes.definition: The necessary paperwork and information required for loan processing.

nodes.label: SBA Response Time
nodes.definition: The time taken by the SBA to respond to loan applications.

nodes.label: SBA Guarantee
nodes.definition: The percentage of the loan amount that the SBA guarantees.

nodes.label: CDFI
nodes.definition: Community Development Financial Institutions that provide financial services in low-income communities.

nodes.label: Certified Development Company
nodes.definition: Organizations that work with the SBA to provide financing to small businesses.

nodes.label: Microlender
nodes.definition: Lenders that provide small loans to entrepreneurs and small businesses.

nodes.label: SBA 7(a)
nodes.definition: A loan program that provides financial assistance to small businesses.
The maximum loan amount available under the Community Advantage program.

nodes.label: Lender Responsibilities
nodes.definition: Responsibilities and duties of lenders in the SBA loan process.

nodes.label: Credit Analysis
nodes.definition: A thorough evaluation of the borrower's financial situation and creditworthiness.

nodes.label: Loan Closing
nodes.definition: The process of finalizing the loan agreement and ensuring all conditions are met.

nodes.label: Loan Servicing
nodes.definition: Ongoing management and support of the loan after disbursement.

nodes.label: Liquidation
nodes.definition: The process of recovering funds from a defaulted loan.

nodes.label: Commercially Reasonable Manner
nodes.definition: A standard of conduct expected from lenders in managing loans.

nodes.label: Loan Documents
nodes.definition: All necessary paperwork required to finalize and manage the loan.

nodes.label: Insurance Verification
nodes.definition: The process of confirming that required insurance is obtained.

nodes.label: Collateral Perfection
nodes.definition: The act of securing interests in collateral through legal means.

nodes.label: Payment Collection
nodes.definition: The process of collecting loan payments from borrowers.

nodes.label: Financial Performance Monitoring
nodes.definition: Ongoing assessment of the borrower's financial health.

nodes.label: Default Warning Signs
nodes.definition: Indicators that a borrower may be at risk of defaulting on the loan.

nodes.label: Loss Claim Submission
nodes.definition: The process of submitting a claim to the SBA for losses incurred.

nodes.label: SBA E-Tran System
nodes.definition: The electronic system used for reporting loan status to the SBA.

nodes.label: SBA Audits and Reviews
nodes.definition: Regular audits and reviews conducted by the SBA to assess lender performance.

nodes.label: On-site Reviews of Loan Files
nodes.definition: Requirement for lenders to undergo on-site reviews of their loan files.

nodes.label: Portfolio Performance Analysis
nodes.definition: Requirement for lenders to have their portfolio performance analyzed.

nodes.label: Compliance with SBA Standard Operating Procedures
nodes.definition: Requirement for lenders to comply with SBA Standard Operating Procedures.

nodes.label: Verification of Proper Use of Delegated Authority
nodes.definition: Requirement for lenders to verify the proper use of their delegated authority.

nodes.label: Purchase Rate
nodes.definition: Percentage of loans purchased by SBA due to default.

nodes.label: Default Rate
nodes.definition: Rate of defaults compared to the SBA portfolio average.

nodes.label: Timeliness of Reporting and Documentation
nodes.definition: Metric assessing the timeliness of lenders' reporting and documentation.

nodes.label: Compliance Findings
nodes.definition: Findings from compliance reviews conducted by the SBA.

nodes.label: Repair Orders
nodes.definition: Orders requiring lenders to take corrective action for non-compliance.

nodes.label: Probation or Suspension of Lending Authority
nodes.definition: Consequences for lenders that may include probation or suspension of their lending authority.

nodes.label: Reduction in Delegation Level
nodes.definition: Reduction in the level of delegation for lenders, such as from PLP to CLP.

nodes.label: Revocation of SBA Lending Authority
nodes.definition: Complete revocation of a lender's authority to lend under SBA programs.

nodes.label: Denial of Guaranty Purchase
nodes.definition: Denial of the ability to purchase a guaranty, resulting in the lender bearing the loss.

nodes.label: Referral to the SBA Office of Inspector General
nodes.definition: Referral of non-compliant lenders to the SBA Office of Inspector General.

nodes.label: SBA Form 1919
nodes.definition: A form required for each principal to provide borrower information.

nodes.label: IRS Tax Transcripts
nodes.definition: Authorization form (Form 4506-C) to obtain tax transcripts from the IRS.

nodes.label: SBA Form 912
nodes.definition: A form required for each principal to provide a statement of personal history.

nodes.label: Credit Reports
nodes.definition: Reports detailing the credit history of all principals and the business.

nodes.label: Appraisals
nodes.definition: Valuations of real estate collateral.

nodes.label: Environmental Reviews
nodes.definition: Assessments required for real estate transactions.

nodes.label: Evidence of Insurance
nodes.definition: Documentation proving that the business has insurance coverage.

nodes.label: Franchise Agreements
nodes.definition: Contracts that outline the terms of a franchise, required if applicable.

nodes.label: Buy-Sell Agreements
nodes.definition: Contracts that outline the terms for buying and selling ownership interests, required if applicable.

nodes.label: Franchise Loans
nodes.definition: Loans made to franchise businesses that require verification of franchise listing on the SBA Franchise Directory.

nodes.label: Change of Ownership
nodes.definition: A situation where a borrower with an existing SBA loan undergoes a change of ownership requiring SBA approval.

nodes.label: Loan Modifications
nodes.definition: Adjustments to existing loans that may or may not require SBA approval depending on the modification type.

nodes.label: SBA Franchise Directory
nodes.definition: A directory that lists franchises eligible for SBA loans.

nodes.label: Payment Deferrals
nodes.definition: Temporary postponement of loan payments, allowed for up to 6 months.

nodes.label: Maturity Extensions
nodes.definition: Extensions of the loan term within SBA maximum terms.

nodes.label: Collateral Substitution
nodes.definition: Replacing one form of collateral with another in a loan agreement.

nodes.label: Release of Collateral
nodes.definition: The process of removing collateral from a loan agreement.

nodes.label: Assumption by New Borrower
nodes.definition: Transfer of loan obligations to a new borrower.

nodes.label: Major Modifications
nodes.definition: Significant changes to loan terms that require SBA approval.

nodes.label: SBA Lending Reporting Requirements
nodes.definition: Requirements for lenders to report various loan-related data to the SBA.

nodes.label: Loan Origination Data
nodes.definition: Data that lenders must report at the time of loan closing.

nodes.label: Monthly Payment Status
nodes.definition: Status of monthly payments that lenders must report.

nodes.label: Annual Financial Reviews
nodes.definition: Annual reviews required for loans over $350,000.

nodes.label: Default Notifications
nodes.definition: Notifications that must be reported within 60 days of an uncured default.

nodes.label: Liquidation Status Updates
nodes.definition: Updates on the status of loan liquidation that lenders must report.

nodes.label: Loss Claims and Recovery Reports
nodes.definition: Reports on loss claims and recovery that lenders must submit.

nodes.label: SBA Lending Authority
nodes.definition: Authority granted to lenders to operate under SBA lending programs.

nodes.label: Lender Performance Evaluations
nodes.definition: Evaluations of lender performance based on reporting accuracy and timeliness.

nodes.label: Loans Over $350,000
nodes.definition: Loans that require annual financial reviews as part of the reporting requirements.

nodes.label: Ethical Standards
nodes.definition: Standards that SBA lenders must adhere to, ensuring fairness and transparency in loan transactions.

nodes.label: Equal Credit Opportunity Act
nodes.definition: A federal law that prohibits discrimination in lending.

nodes.label: Fair Housing Act
nodes.definition: A federal law that prohibits discrimination in housing-related transactions.

nodes.label: No Conflicts of Interest
nodes.definition: A requirement that lenders must avoid any conflicts of interest in loan transactions.

nodes.label: Fair Treatment of Applicants
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to treat all loan applicants fairly and equally.

nodes.label: Transparency in Fees
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to be transparent about fees and charges associated with loans.

nodes.label: Protection of Borrower Information
nodes.definition: A requirement for lenders to protect the personal information of borrowers.

nodes.label: Prohibition on Referral Fees
nodes.definition: A requirement that prohibits lenders from accepting referral fees or kickbacks.
